item.title: Automating Instagram Without the Instagram API
item.description:
Automating Instagram Without the Instagram API

The Instagram API exists. I would not recommend it.

To post a single photo programmatically, Meta requires: a Facebook Page, a connected Instagram Business account, a developer app, an approved instagram_content_publish permission, and an access token that expires. Miss any one of those and you get a 400 error with a reference code that links to documentation last updated in 2021.

We needed to automate social posting for Cyberforks. We spent two hours on the API path before deciding life is short and browsers are programmable.

item.title: cyber for breakfast. shitpost for dinner.
item.description:
Every security practitioner eventually gets tired of the noise — the endless stream of CVE notifications, patch Tuesdays, and vendor webinars that promise to solve the problem of humans being humans.

So we built something.

This blog is where Cyberforks documents what it actually looks like to automate the boring parts, build AI-assisted workflows for real security work, and occasionally meme about the absurdity of it all.

Some posts will be technical. Some will be sarcastic. All of them will be real — pulled from actual work, actual incidents, actual moments of “why does this not exist yet?”

item.title: about
item.description:
Cyberforks LLC is a security consulting shop that does the work that actually matters — IR planning, compliance assessments, and occasionally explaining to executives why “password1!” is not a strategy.

This blog documents tools, automations, and lessons learned from the field. Some technical. Some sarcastic. All real.

→ cyberforks.com
